What Is Surfaced to Human: Formatted summary of all three analyses presented together: (1) Ticket analysis — score / trend / volume vs previous quarter / key issues / SLA compliance / detailed findings. (2) Usage analysis — score / trend / feature adoption rates / detailed findings. (3) Sentiment analysis — score / summary / key threads flagged for attention. Data is formatted for review readability — not raw JSON. Each dimension shows its score / supporting evidence / and areas of concern.
Expected Human Input: Freeform text — the human can: confirm the analyses are correct (e.g. 'Looks good'); correct specific scores or findings (e.g. 'Ticket score should be Amber not Green — the SLA breach on P1 tickets is more significant than the volume suggests'); add missing context the agent lacks (e.g. 'This account switched support channels mid-quarter so ticket volume is artificially low'); flag data quality issues (e.g. 'Usage data looks stale — check the API response date'). No structured format required.
Routing After Resume: Unconditional edge → synthesise. The graph always moves forward to synthesise regardless of what the human says. The human's input shapes what the synthesise node knows / not which node runs next.
Decision Options: N/A — freeform input with no structured decision. The human is enriching context not making a routing decision.
Design Rationale: The human reviews related dimensions of the same account together — presenting three separate interrupts for tickets / usage / and sentiment would force context-switching and prevent cross-referencing. Feedback-as-Context is correct because the human enriches the next node's reasoning (corrections flow into the synthesis prompt as additional context) without changing the graph's path. The synthesise node always runs after this checkpoint. An upstream validation here also enables Consolidation Principle 2: Steps 8-9 (synthesis) can be automated because the human has already confirmed the inputs.

What Is Surfaced to Human: Complete report package: (1) Full report_markdown with all seven sections (Account Overview / Ticket Health / Product Engagement / Sentiment / Risks & Opportunities / Health Score / Recommended Actions). (2) exec_summary — the 3-5 sentence executive summary. (3) health_score and health_justification displayed prominently. (4) risks and opportunities lists. The interrupt payload presents explicit decision options: approve / revise / rework — with descriptions of what each option does.
Expected Human Input: Structured response with two components: (1) A decision value — one of 'approved' / 'revise' / 'rework'. (2) Optional instructions string — required for revise and rework / not needed for approved. For revise: specific feedback about what to change in the report (e.g. 'Soften the language in the Risk section — the client relationship is sensitive' or 'Add more detail to the Recommended Actions'). For rework: instructions for what to re-analyse (e.g. 'The sentiment score is wrong — re-read the Slack threads from February focusing on the billing complaint thread').
State Fields Written: report_review_decision (always written); report_human_feedback (written on revise path — consumed by generate_report); rework_instructions (written on rework path — consumed by analyse_health)
Routing After Resume: Conditional edges based on report_review_decision: (1) 'approved' → END — the report is final and the graph completes. (2) 'revise' → generate_report — only the report is regenerated using report_human_feedback as revision instructions. Analysis and synthesis are preserved. (3) 'rework' → analyse_health — the analysis is re-run from scratch using rework_instructions as a focus-area guide. Synthesis and report generation also re-run downstream.
Decision Options: approved — the report meets quality standards and is ready for the MANUAL distribution step (Step 12 / outside graph). revise — the report has issues that can be fixed by regenerating the report text without changing the underlying analysis (e.g. tone / structure / missing detail). rework — the underlying analysis is flawed and needs to be re-run before a new report can be generated (e.g. a score is wrong / a key finding is missing / sentiment was misinterpreted).
Design Rationale: This is a quality gate where the human may redirect execution — the defining characteristic of Feedback-as-Control-Flow. Three decision options give the reviewer proportional control: minor wording issues use the lightweight revise path (regenerate report only) while fundamental analysis errors use the heavier rework path (re-run analysis + synthesis + report). This prevents the reviewer from having to accept a flawed report or reject it entirely with no middle ground. The routing function validates the decision value to prevent silent misrouting.
